playbooks.title: Plugin load failure triage
playbooks.summary: The plugin failed before it became usable. Confirm deployment, manifest identity, and the earliest stack trace before changing unrelated UI code.

commands.label: Generate a doctor fix plan
commands.summary: Capture missing build/deploy/CLI/CDP prerequisites and write a reviewable fix script next to the doctor report.

actions: Compare the repo manifest, dist manifest, and test vault manifest before changing source code.
Use the earliest stack trace after reload as the root symptom.
Rebuild and redeploy generated artifacts before retrying plugin reload.

playbooks.title: Obsidian app-control recovery
playbooks.summary: CLI or CDP automation could not reach the target Obsidian desktop instance. Fix the app-control surface before trusting plugin diagnostics.

commands.label: Probe CDP targets
commands.summary: List exposed CDP targets before retrying CDP automation.
args: const response = await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list'); console.log(await response.text());

actions: Launch Obsidian desktop and open or focus the target test vault before retrying CLI commands.
If using CDP, restart Obsidian with a debug port and filter by vault title in multi-window setups.
Do not diagnose plugin code until app-control preflight succeeds.

playbooks.title: Visible UI performance triage
playbooks.summary: Startup or view-open timing crossed a generic budget. Split startup, view construction, render, hydration, and background refresh before changing code.

commands.label: Rebuild the HTML report
commands.summary: Produce a portable diagnosis report before comparing warm/cold timing classes.

actions: Record startup-complete and view-open milestones in the same run.
Render a usable shell before non-critical enrichment work where safe.
Save separate cold-start and warm-start baselines so app variance does not hide regressions.

playbooks.title: Artifact capture recovery
playbooks.summary: Screenshot or DOM artifacts are missing, so the run is not trustworthy enough for UI debugging yet.

commands.label: Retry CLI screenshot capture
commands.summary: Capture a fresh screenshot directly through the active Obsidian CLI command.

commands.label: Retry CLI DOM capture
commands.summary: Re-query the suspected plugin root selector before trusting downstream UI assertions.

commands.label: Capture UI through CDP
commands.summary: Fallback capture path when CLI screenshot or DOM collection still misses the active plugin leaf.

actions: Verify the selector targets the plugin pane instead of the previously active leaf.
Use a scenario or CDP post-reload eval to focus/open the plugin view before capture.
Do not trust downstream assertions until both DOM and screenshot capture succeed.
